---
title: "Mason Reece"
pagetitle: "Mason Reece"
page-layout: custom
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    css: assets/index.css
    toc: false
---

::: {.profile-layout}

::: {.profile-sidebar}
![](assets/mason.jpeg){.profile-photo}

# Mason Reece

PhD Candidate in Political Science, MIT

[mpreece (at) mit (dot) edu](mailto:mpreece@mit.edu){.profile-email}
[Curriculum Vitae](files/reece-cv.pdf){.profile-email}

::: {.profile-links}
[{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/mreece13 "Personal GitHub")
[{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.mit.edu/mpreece "MIT GitHub")
[{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://www.linkedin.com/in/mason-reece/ "LinkedIn")
[{{< ai google-scholar >}}](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=XGy8x8cAAAAJ&hl=en "Google Scholar")
:::

:::

::: {.profile-content}

Hi, I'm a PhD candidate in MIT's Political Science department and an affiliate with [MIT's Election Data + Science Lab](https://electionlab.mit.edu/). I'm primarily interested in the behavior of voters and political actors at the state and local level in the United States. I also collect and develop methods to analyze large datasets. My dissertation uses ballot-level data (cast vote records) to study representation and competition in politics.

<!-- My work has been published or is forthcoming in  -->

To bring a practical lens to my research, I have served as the lead poll worker in Houston and Cambridge and organized a youth voters conference. In my free time, I love riding my bike, hiking, and trying food from around the world.

## Working Papers

- Mason Reece. "Politics Without the Fight: The Consequences of Uncontested Elections"

- Mason Reece. "[Even Faster Ideal Point Estimation: Using Variational Autoencoders to Estimate Item-Response Theory Models](files/vaes.pdf)"

- Mason Reece and Daniel M. Thompson. "[Partisan Elections and Partisan Voting: Evidence from Partisan Judicial Elections](files/partisan_judges.pdf)." *Under review.*

- Adam Rauh, Mason Reece, and Tyler Simko. "[Local community opposition to data centers is clear and growing](files/dcenters.pdf)." *Under review.*

- Shigeo Hirano, Joseph R. Loffredo, Mason Reece, and James M. Snyder Jr. "Trends in Nationalization of State and Local Elections."

## Publications

- Shiro Kuriwaki, Mason Reece, Samuel Baltz, Aleksandra Conevska, Joseph R. Loffredo, Can E. Mutlu, Taran Samarth, Kevin E. Acevedo Jetter, Zachary Djanogly Garai, Kate Murray, Shigeo Hirano, Jeffrey B. Lewis, James M. Snyder Jr., and Charles H. Stewart III (2024). "[Cast vote records: A database of ballots from the 2020 U.S. Election](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41597-024-04017-1)." *Nature Scientific Data.* Co-first author with Kuriwaki. [Github](https://github.com/kuriwaki/cvr_harvard-mit_scripts). [Dataverse](https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/PQQ3KV).

- Lonna Atkeson, M. V. Hood III, Colin Jones, Mason Reece, and Robert M. Stein (2022). "[Postal Voting in the 2020 Election](https://electioncenter.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/JEARP-Volume-01-Issue-01.pdf)." *Journal of Election Administration, Research and Practice.*

## Datasets

- Shiro Kuriwaki, Mason Reece, Samuel Baltz, Aleksandra Conevska, Joseph R. Loffredo, Can E. Mutlu, Taran Samarth, Kevin E. Acevedo Jetter, Zachary Djanogly Garai, Kate Murray, Shigeo Hirano, Jeffrey B. Lewis, James M. Snyder Jr., and Charles H. Stewart III (2024). "Cast vote records: A database of ballots from the 2020 U.S. Election." [Dataverse](https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/PQQ3KV)

- Soubhik Barari, Tyler Simko, Mason Reece (2026). "LocalView Public Meetings Database" [Dataverse](https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/NJTBEM)

- Tyler Simko, Rebecca Johnson, Mirya Holman, Mason Reece (2026). "DistrictView School Board Meetings Database" [Dataverse](https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/ERDATA)

## Teaching

### Training

- [Kaufman Teaching Certificate](https://tll.mit.edu/programming/grad-student-programming/kaufman-teaching-certificate-program/). MIT Teaching + Learning Lab. 2026.

### Experience
- TA for Generalized Linear Modeling and Bayesian Inference. MIT. 2025. Evaluation from students: 7.0/7.0. Professor: In Song Kim.
- Reading (and writing) Quantitative Journal Articles. [MIT's Pathways to Political Science Workshop](https://globaldiversitylab.mit.edu/summer-research-internship/). 2026.
- Introduction to Remote Resources. MIT's Political Methodology Lab Workshop. 2025. Co-instructor with [Gabrielle Péloquin-Skulski](https://gskulski.com/)
- Introduction to Remote Resources. MIT's Political Methodology Lab Workshop. 2024.

:::

:::
